```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  xv <- -2:2; raw <- sample(1:9,5,replace=TRUE); pr <- raw/sum(raw)
  y <- sample(1:2,1); ans <- sum(pr[abs(xv)==y])
  vals <- round(c(ans,pr[xv==y],pr[xv==-y],sum(pr[abs(xv)<=y]),1-ans),4)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals); ptxt <- paste(sprintf("%.3f",pr),collapse=", ")
```

Question
========
Uma variável tem suporte $(-2,-1,0,1,2)$ e probabilidades $(`r ptxt`)$. Se $Y=|X|$, qual é $P(Y=`r y`)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
As pré-imagens de $Y=`r y`$ são $X=-`r y`$ e $X=`r y`$. Logo,
$$P(Y=`r y`)=P(X=-`r y`)+P(X=`r y`)
=`r sprintf("%.4f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S6Q6_transformacao_valor_absoluto
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
